normalized_content: GitHub issue: [PRD] Support non-interactive CLI mode URL: https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands-CLI/issues/147 Number: 147 State: closed State reason: completed Created at: 2025-08-20T16:33:01Z Updated at: 2025-12-29T22:30:19Z Closed at: 2025-12-29T22:30:19Z Labels: enhancement, roadmap Body: ## Problems to solve * Current Headless mode is not at parity with [OpenHands CLI](https://docs.all-hands.dev/usage/how-to/cli-mode) * No MCP support * Designed originally for debugging OpenHands; but not for building scalable automation/scripting * Users want a non-interactive CLI experience with OpenHands, but the current Headless mode is difficult to install, hard to use * Hard to configure * Requires docker and following development instructions * Many users don’t have `tmux` installed ## Customer / User evidence * Some customers have built custom integrations and interact with OpenHands through the existing websocket connection. * Several open source users have built custom automation around OpenHands, but have made significant changes or forks to make this possible. * Interviews with Open Source users have indicated they'd prefer to start with a non-interactive CLI mode instead of the current Terminal UI. These users prefer to build custom automation that allows them to run multiple conversations at once. ## Goals * easy to install * ability to run as a command line * works on Windows, Mac, and Linux environments ## **Requirements** * Should not require docker (already accomplished with current CLI) * After a command is entered, display a counter/spinner to show the user the CLI is working * Allows for users to start conversations from the command line into the Terminal UI * `openhands "start a convo"` * Allows users to run one-off prompts - outputs as text (max verbosity) * `openhands exec "do a flip"` * Allows users to resume a local conversation into the Terminal UI * `openhands resume <GUID>` * Resume last conversation * `openhands resume --last` * List available conversations to resume * `openhands resume` * Ability to change output format as an argument (json, text \[default\]) * `openhands exec "do a flip" --output json` * Ability to configure LLM auto-approve * #107 **Nice to have** * Ability to call and use microagents from prompts * Ability to delegate a task to OpenHands Cloud (depends on https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands-Cloud/issues/205) * `openhands -cloud -p 'do a flip'`
title: OpenHands CLI non-interactive mode PRD
